title: Polypodium abbreviatum
taxonomy: Polypodium abbreviatum belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. The species Polypodium abbreviatum is a type of terrestrial fern that is native to certain regions. It is classified as a member of the genus Polypodium, which contains various species of polypody ferns. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns. Polypodium abbreviatum's classification also helps in understanding its ecological and geographical distribution.

intro: Polypodium abbreviatum, also known as Short-hair Polypody, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Polypodium abbreviatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
